channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Chernobyl's radioactive landscape is a testament to nature’s resilience and survival spirit
item.description: Wildlife is thriving again four decades after the nuclear disaster at Ukraine’s Chernobyl power plant in what became the exclusion zone created by the forced mass evacuations of the population.
item.dc:creator: Derek Gatopoulos And Evgeniy Maloletka, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House chief of staff meets with Anthropic CEO over its new AI technology
item.description: White House chief of staff Susie Wiles has met with Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei to discuss the company's new AI model, Mythos.
item.dc:creator: Josh Boak And Matt O'Brien, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Robot Zuckerberg shows how IT can free up CEOs’ time
item.description: Mark Zuckerberg, the CEO of Meta, is building an AI version of himself. The virtual CEO is being trained on Zuckerberg’s mannerisms and will be loaded with his views on corporate strategy, the Financial Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Oracle delivers semantic search without LLMs
item.description: Oracle says its new Trusted Answer Search can deliver reliable results at scale in the enterprise by scouring a governed set of approved documents using vector search instead of large language models (LLMs) and retrieval-augmented generation (RAG).
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI anxiety is turning volatile
item.description: Welcome to AI Decoded, Fast Company’s weekly newsletter that breaks down the most important news in the world of AI. You can sign up to receive this newsletter every week via email here.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sullivan, Fast Company

item.title: Inside NTT Research’s push to commercialize deep tech
item.description: Since opening in Silicon Valley in 2019, NTT Research has operated as a long-horizon science lab, a dedicated arm of Japan’s telecommunications giant NTT Group, which invests more than $3 billion annually in global R&D.
item.dc:creator: Victor Dey, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft to cut Windows 365 price for SMBs
item.description: Microsoft will cut the price of Windows 365 subscriptions for small and mid-sized businesses by 20% next month, though analysts expect little impact on uptake of the Desktop-as-a-Service (DaaS) platform.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Curity looks to reinvent IAM with runtime authorization for AI agents
item.description: In 2026, enterprise developers are building and deploying the first generation of powerful, increasingly autonomous AI agents at incredible speed. Now comes the hard part: working out how to secure them. Vendors in the space are facing multiple challenges.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: GitHub adds Stacked PRs to speed complex code reviews
item.description: AI-aided development tools are churning out more lines of code than ever, presenting a challenge for reviewers who must review ever larger pull requests.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Critical flaw in Marimo Python notebook exploited within 10 hours of disclosure
item.description: A critical pre-authentication remote code execution vulnerability in Marimo, an open-source Python notebook platform owned by AI cloud company CoreWeave, was exploited in the wild less than 10 hours after its public disclosure, according to the Sysdig Threat Research Team.
item.dc:creator: Info World
